site.blogLabel: Liner notes & full essay: Learning from the Failures of Others
site.disclosure: Human: concept, themes, art direction, curation. AI: lyric drafting, vocals, instrumentation — all prompted and shaped by the human.

band.name: Normalization of Deviance
band.tagline: Concept albums about the operational sins that murder throughput.

altEgos.name: Throughput Mojo
altEgos.description: Cowpunk alter ego. Same lyrics, different bar.

albums.title: Full Kit Rock
albums.subtitle: A CCPM Concept Album
albums.tagline: Eli Goldratt's Critical Chain set to driving rock with the urgency his ideas deserve.
albums.description: Eleven tracks teaching the operating system of throughput-driven projects: avoid bad multitasking, verify the full kit, triage the constraint, synchronise the relay, increase the dosage, abolish local optima. Critical Chain Project Management, but you can hum it.

essay.title: TOC's Critical Chain and Rules of Flow
essay.label: Read the deeper story behind the album

linerNotes.concept: Bonus — The Question Is the Mechanism

tracks.title: The Whole Is the Goal
tracks.subtitle: (The Whole Damn System)
tracks.oneliner: Local optimisations don't strengthen the chain. The whole is the goal.
tracks.description: We open with the big idea: the goal isn't for your team to finish faster or for your department to look good. It's for the entire system to deliver more. Local optimisations don't strengthen the chain.
tracks.audioSource: full-kit-rock/The Whole is the Goal (The Whole Damn System).mp3

tracks.title: Stop Starting, Start Finishing
tracks.oneliner: Too much WIP kills flow. Finish what you started.
tracks.description: Too much work-in-progress kills flow. Four people running eight sprint tickets at once means nothing actually ships. Control WIP. Finish before you start.

tracks.title: Fix the Blocker, Not the Noise
tracks.oneliner: Triage by what unblocks flow, not by what shouts loudest.
tracks.description: When everything is urgent, how do you decide what to do first? Triage isn't about volume or politics; it's about picking the work that unblocks flow. Address the real constraint instead of the loudest complaint.
tracks.audioSource: full-kit-rock/Fix the Blocker, Not the Noise.mp3

tracks.title: Full Kit First
tracks.subtitle: (Show Me the Full Kit)
tracks.oneliner: Don't start a task you can't finish. Names, decisions, specs, test env — show me the full kit.
tracks.description: Ever started a DIY project and realised halfway through that you're missing parts? That's life without a full kit. A pre-flight checklist: names, decisions, specs, test environment — everything you need before starting so you don't stall mid-task.
tracks.audioSource: full-kit-rock/Full Kit First (Show Me the Full Kit).mp3

tracks.title: No More Secret Safety
tracks.oneliner: Hidden safety in every task gets eaten. Pool the buffer.
tracks.description: Padding every task with extra time doesn't make projects safer; it invites procrastination and polishing. Hidden safety gets eaten. Pooled buffers fix the problem.

tracks.title: Your Schedule Is Lying
tracks.subtitle: (Build the Real Chain)
tracks.oneliner: Gantt charts pretend one person can be in three places. Count the resources, build the real chain.
tracks.description: A traditional Gantt chart ignores resource availability. It assumes the same person can be in three places at once. Build the critical chain by counting scarce resources and dependencies — no fantasy scheduling.
tracks.audioSource: full-kit-rock/Your Schedule is Lying (Build the real chain).mp3

tracks.title: Percent Complete Is a Lie
tracks.subtitle: (Show Me the Buffer)
tracks.oneliner: '90% done' is a comforting fiction. Show me the buffer burn.
tracks.description: You've heard '90% done' too many times. Use the buffer burn and remaining duration to track progress. Percent complete is for suckers; fever charts are your friend.
tracks.audioSource: full-kit-rock/Percent complete is a lie (show me the buffer).mp3

tracks.title: Don't Break the Relay
tracks.subtitle: (Done Means the Next Step Can Run)
tracks.oneliner: Critical chain is a relay race. Don't drop the baton.
tracks.description: Once the critical chain is in motion, it should move like a relay race: start when the baton arrives, finish without delay, and hand it off cleanly. Resource buffers and synchronised work so finished tasks don't sit waiting.

tracks.title: Increase the Dosage
tracks.subtitle: (The Dose Isn't Strong Enough)
tracks.oneliner: If the problem keeps coming back, the dose isn't strong enough.
tracks.description: Ever fix a problem only to have it return like a villain in a horror movie? Sometimes the remedy is too weak. Increase the dosage — give the issue more time, more expertise, or a stronger gate.

tracks.title: Stop Paying Twice
tracks.oneliner: Rework is work going backwards. Standardise the traps; protect the judgment.
tracks.description: Rework is work going backwards. When requirements change after coding or tests are written after bugs, you're paying twice. Standardise the repetitive traps while leaving room for judgment where it creates value.

tracks.title: Ask Until They See It
tracks.subtitle: (The Question Is the Mechanism)
tracks.oneliner: People defend processes you hand them. They fight for conclusions they discover.
tracks.description: Goldratt's novels rarely preach; they ask questions that guide characters to discover the answers. Instead of lecturing people about constraints, ask where work is waiting, what happens when safety is hidden, what one expert on five projects does to a schedule.
tracks.audioSource: full-kit-rock/Ask Until They See It (The Question Is the Mechanism).mp3

albums.title: Sins Against Throughput
albums.subtitle: Concept EP — Seven Sins
albums.tagline: Hardcore nerdcore for operators who worship throughput.
albums.description: Seven tracks. Seven failure modes that quietly murder flow inside otherwise functional organizations. Hit play. Pull the cord.

tracks.title: Stop the Line
tracks.oneliner: Serious defects deserve an andon-cord response. Focus stays until truly resolved.
tracks.description: Serious defects should trigger an andon-cord full response. Focus should remain until truly resolved.

alternateVersions.oneliner: Same sin, hung over and unrepentant. Andon cord meets last call at Lonely Junction.
alternateVersions.description: A country/roadhouse reinterpretation by the band's alter ego, Throughput Mojo, off the EP Bottleneck Saloon. Same lyrics, different barstool.
alternateVersions.audioSource: Stop the Line (Normalization of Deviance) - Roadhouse version.mp3
alternateVersions.artSource: Stop the line (Normalization of Deviance) - Roadhouse - Mojo.png

tracks.title: No North Star
tracks.oneliner: Without top-down alignment, local and short-term decisions fill the gap.
tracks.description: Lacking top-down alignment on the few things that need to be true in the future. Local and short-term decisions will fill the gap.

alternateVersions.oneliner: Compass spins past Lonely Junction. The vacuum of intent goes country.
alternateVersions.description: A country/roadhouse reinterpretation by the band's alter ego, Throughput Mojo, off the EP Bottleneck Saloon. Cold beer, bad decisions, so many avoidable regrets.
alternateVersions.audioSource: No North Star (Vacuum of Intent) - Country mix.mp3
alternateVersions.artSource: No North Star (Vacuum of Intent) - Roadhouse.png

tracks.title: Coordination Spaghetti
tracks.oneliner: When too many people must talk to too many others for too many decisions.
tracks.description: A risk when many people need to talk to too many other people for many decisions.

tracks.title: Patched Not Fixed
tracks.oneliner: Managing via expediting instead of fixing the underlying constraint.
tracks.description: Managing via expediting instead of fixing underlying constraints.

tracks.title: Few Nodes, Many Consequences
tracks.oneliner: A small number of subject-matter experts limit progress. Have a plan for the bottleneck.
tracks.description: A small number of subject matter experts limit progress. If work piles up in front of these bottlenecks it's key to have a plan. This is true about all bottlenecks — but often especially ignored with people.
tracks.audioSource: Few Nodes, Many Consequences (Fragility of the Few).mp3

tracks.title: Now Now Now
tracks.oneliner: Picking the short-term better over the long-term valuable. Again. And again.
tracks.description: Consistently choosing the short-term better over the long-term. Long term is harder, but more valuable.
tracks.audioSource: Now Now Now (Tyranny of the Urgent).mp3

tracks.title: Docs or It Didn't Happen
tracks.oneliner: If knowledge stays in people instead of shared systems, the org has memory loss.
tracks.description: A risk if people talk when they should write. Knowledge stays in people instead of in shared systems.
tracks.audioSource: Docs or it didn't happen (Oral Tradition).mp3
